segments.s648-g377: همی‌گفت is the frequentive/imperfective past form (همی + گفت) meaning “kept saying” or “was saying,” indicating repeated or ongoing speech. بی‌رنج is an adjectival phrase (bi- ‘without’ + رنج ‘suffering’) modifying تخت; Persian often places such modifiers before the noun in poetry. این follows تخت as the demonstrative “this,” and بود is the past copula (“was”) linking the predicate to the subject; the overall order is poetic but translates as “he kept saying that this throne was free of suffering.”
segments.s648-g378: که is the subordinate/complementizer “that,” introducing the content of what was said. بی‌کوشش (bi- + کوشش) means “without effort” or “effortless”; the coordinated items joined by و list the negatives (درد ‘pain/suffering’ and نفرین ‘curse’). The clause closes with بود, the past copula (“was”), a typical placement at the end in classical/poetic Persian.
segments.s648-g57: Word order is somewhat inverted in the poem: literal order 'From head took up troop hat' means 'the troop lifted their hats from their heads' (they removed them). 'ز' is the poetic preposition 'from', 'سر' is the body-part 'head', 'برگرفتند' is past plural 'they lifted/took off', 'گردان' is the subject 'troop', and 'کلاه' is the object 'hat'.
segments.s648-g58: 'به ماتم نشستند' is an idiomatic phrase: 'نشستند' (they sat/settled) with the preposition 'به' meaning 'into/toward' expresses entering a state — 'they sat down into mourning'. 'با سوگ شاه' supplements it: 'with the lament/mourning for the king.' Note: the lemma for 'نشستند' given in the data seems mismatched; here it is the past of 'نشستن' = 'they sat'.
segments.s648-g59: 'همی‌گفت' is an iterative past (kept saying/kept on saying). 'بر' here marks direction/target ('against' or 'toward') so 'بر کینه شهریار' means 'out of/with hatred toward the king' or 'concerning the king's grudge.' 'کینه' = 'hatred/grudge' and 'شهریار' = 'the monarch/king'.
segments.s648-g60: This is a nominal phrase describing the claimant: 'بلاش جوان' = 'Balash the young (man)'. 'چون بود خواستار' is roughly 'how he was a claimant' or 'in what way he was desirous/claimant' — 'چون' = 'how', 'بود' = past tense 'was', and 'خواستار' = 'desirous/claimant'. The line reports people saying that Balash was eager to claim the throne.
segments.s648-g61: 'بدانست' is perfect/past 'he knew/realized'. 'کان' functions as a demonstrative/linker here ('that' / 'so that'), introducing the clause 'کار بی‌سود شد' = 'the affair became fruitless'. 'بی‌سود' = 'without gain/useless', and 'شد' = past 'became'.
segments.s648-g62: This clause describes a figurative result: 'سر تاج شاهی' = 'the top/head of the kingship-crown' (سر = summit/top, تاج = crown, شاهی = of kingship/royal). 'پر از دود شد' = 'became full of smoke' (پر = full (of), از = 'of/from' in the 'full of' construction, دود = smoke, شد = became).
segments.s648-g63: 'سپاه پراگنده را گرد کرد' is active: 'he made the scattered army gather' — 'سپاه' = army (object marked by 'را'), 'پراگنده' = scattered, 'گرد' here conveys the sense 'around/into a group' and with 'کرد' (to make/do) forms 'gathered'. Word order places the object before the verb as is common in Persian poetry.
segments.s648-g64: This is a compact action sequence: 'بزد کوس' = 'he struck/beat the war-drum' (past). 'وز دشت برخاست گرد' = 'a gust/from the plain rose up; dust/whirl rose' — 'وز' = a blast/gust (here instrumental 'a blast from the plain'), 'دشت' = plain, 'برخاست' = rose up, and the final 'گرد' functions as 'dust/whirl' (a cloud of dust).
segments.s648-g153: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when' (introducing a temporal clause). پیروز is an adjective 'victorious'. باشد is the classical 3rd-person present/subjunctive copula 'is' (here marking the state). به + دشت forms a locative phrase 'on/into the plain' and نبرد names the battlefield ('battle') and follows as a genitival/epithet: 'the plain of battle.'
segments.s648-g154: شکسته is a past-participle/adjectival use 'broken' or 'having been broken' (describing the defeated opposition or the state after breaking). بکنده is an older/poetic verbal form meaning 'he tears/pulls out' (a verbal action following the participle). درون 'inside' is the direct object, modified by the following phrase پر ز گرد = 'full of dust' (پر = 'full (of)', ز = poetic 'of/from', گرد = 'dust').
segments.s648-g155: گر is the conditional 'if'. آیی is the 2nd-person present/subjunctive 'you come' (classical word form). تو is the pronoun 'you'. ایدر 'here' (literary). هم means 'also/even' (emphatic), and آراستست is a 3rd-person present 'is adorned/arrayed'—so the clause reads 'If you come here, this place is also adorned.'
segments.s648-g156: نه ... و نه ... is the correlative negative pair 'neither ... nor ...'. جنگ 'war/battle' and جنگ‌آوران 'warriors' are the two coordinated nouns. کاستست is an old 3rd-person copular/impersonal form meaning 'was lacking' or 'there was none'—so the sentence denies the presence of war or warriors.
segments.s648-g157: فرستاده is the noun 'messenger' (agent). با + نامه is an instrumental/associative phrase 'with a letter'. تازان here is a poetic adjective describing speed/haste (literally 'swift/fierce' in the sense of hurrying). ز جای 'from the place' gives the origin. Word order places the accompanying/adiunct phrase before the time clause that follows.
segments.s648-g158: به یک هفته is a temporal expression 'in/within one week' (به + numeral + time noun). آمد is the past tense verb 'came'. سوی + اسم is a directional phrase 'toward Soofzā' (سوفزای is a proper name in the oblique case). The classical order places the time expression before the verb.
segments.s648-g159: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. برخواند here is the past tense 'read aloud' / 'recited' (classical verb meaning to read/announce). آن نامه را is the definite object marked by را ('that letter' as object). پهلوان is the subject 'the hero/champion' placed after the object, a typical poetic/inverted order.
segments.s648-g160: به دشنام is a prepositional phrase 'with insults/curse-words' (instrument/means). بگشاد is an archaic/past verbal form 'opened' used figuratively with زبان ('opened his tongue' = 'began to speak'). گویا modifies manner — here best read as 'expressive/eloquent' or 'spoken aloud' — and زبان 'tongue' means 'speech/utterance'. Together: 'he opened his tongue with curses and spoke.'
segments.s648-g241: اسیران (‘the captives’) is the subject. وز here is the fused form of و + ز (classical/poetic), functioning as a linker meaning “and/of” that connects the following noun phrase to اسیران. خواسته هرچ بود is a relative clause: خواسته (‘what was asked for/desired’) + هرچ (‘whatever’) + بود (past copula), so literally “whatever (they) had wanted/asked for.”
segments.s648-g242: ز (poetic preposition) introduces the list of items “from/among.” The sequence سیم و زر و گوهر lists valuables (silver, gold, jewel). نابسود is a prefixed adjective with نـ negating a root meaning ‘to be profited/fulfilled’; in context it qualifies the previous noun-phrase and means roughly “not left unsatisfied/ not without gain.”
segments.s648-g243: ز introduces items taken/concerned “from”: ز اسب و سلیح (from horses and weapons) and then ز تاج و ز تخت repeats the preposition for the next pair (poetic repetition for rhythm). Word order is a simple coordinated list joined by و.
segments.s648-g244: که introduces a relative clause (“which/that”), آن روز (“that day”) is the time phrase, and بگذاشت is a past narrative verb meaning “he put aside/relinquished/left” (classical past), with پیروزبخت as an epithet postposed to the verb meaning “the fortunate one.” The clause modifies the previous list (i.e., those items were relinquished that day by the fortunate one).
segments.s648-g245: فرستم is an archaic/narrative verb form here meaning “(he) sent” (classical epic past form). همه (‘all/everything’) is the direct object. نزد means “to, into the presence of” and سالار شاه is the indirect object ‘the king’s commander’ (title + possessor). Word order is Verb–Object–IndirectObject, a common classical arrangement.
segments.s648-g246: This is a coordinated list of the things sent: سراپرده (inner tent/inner chamber), گنج (treasure), پیل (elephants) and سپاه (army). The repeated و simply links the items; the whole phrase is the object content enumerated after فرستم همه نزد سالار شاه.
segments.s648-g247: چو is the temporal/conditional conjunction “when” (classical usage). پیروزگر is a vocative/description ‘victor’ or ‘conqueror.’ سوی ایران شوی uses سوی + place + شوی (2nd-person form of شدن) meaning “you become/arrive toward Iran” — so the clause reads “when you are victorious toward/return to Iran.” The verb شوی is a present/subjunctive form addressing ‘you’.
segments.s648-g248: به نزدیک = “to/into the proximity of,” شاه دلیران is a genitival phrase ‘the king of the brave’ (دلیران plural used as a noun). شوی repeats the verb “you will be / become” (future/inevitable sense in classical narrative). Altogether: “you will be near the king of the brave.”
segments.s648-g105: نیای تو (niyâ-ye to) = 'your ancestor' with the possessive pronoun تو following the noun. زین is an archaic/prepositional element meaning 'of/from this' that links to خاندان ('family, house'), and زنده بود is the predicate 'was alive' (زنده = adjective, بود = past copula). Word order is Subject (نیای تو) — modifier (زین خاندان) — predicate (زنده بود).
segments.s648-g106: پدر (subject 'the father/forefather') is followed by the adverbial/prepositional phrase پیش بهرام ('before/earlier than Bahram'), and پاینده بود is the adjectival predicate 'was long‑lived' (پاینده = long‑lived/everlasting, بود = past copula). The clause states a past fact with the adjective after the noun, a common Persian order.
segments.s648-g107: من اینک به مرو آمدم is a simple past / perfective statement 'I have come to Merv now' (من = I, اینک = now, به مرو = to Merv, آمدم = I came). The word کینه‌خواه follows as a predicative/appositive noun meaning 'avenger' or 'one who seeks vengeance' — 'I have come to Merv as an avenger.'
segments.s648-g108: نماند is a past form meaning 'there remained/was left' (here with a negative force: 'no longer remains'); به هیتالیان marks the affected party 'for the Hitalians'; تاج و گاه are the coordinated nouns 'crown and time' (i.e. sovereignty and opportunity). Word order places the verbal predicate first, then the indirect object and the coordinated objects.
segments.s648-g109: اسیران و آن خواسته هرچ هست lists items: 'the captives and that requested/claimed — whatever there is.' هرچ هست ('whatever there is') is a free relative quantifier following the noun phrase, with هست as the copula/existential verb at the end.
segments.s648-g110: که introduces a relative clause 'that/which'. از رزمگاه is the source 'from the battlefield'. آمدستت is an archaic/contracted verbal form meaning 'has come to you' (آمده است + ت → آمدستت), and بدست ('in/into the hand') indicates possession — together: 'which has come into your hands from the battlefield.'
segments.s648-g111: همه is the object 'all/everyone'. بازخواهم is a 1st‑person future/factive verb 'I will reclaim/recover' (بازخواه + ـم). به شمشیر کین marks the instrument/means 'by the sword of vengeance' where کین qualifies شمشیر (genitive/attributive relationship).
segments.s648-g112: به مرو آورم is 'I will bring to Merv' (به مرو = to Merv, آورم = I will bring, present/subjunctive used as future). خاک توران زمین is a poetic apposition meaning 'the soil/earth of Turan' (خاک = soil, توران = Turan, زمین appositive 'land/country'). The object follows the verb in this order for emphasis.
segments.s648-g137: که here is the relative/continuative particle 'that/for' linking this clause to what precedes. هر کس که is a standard correlative 'whoever' (هر + کس + که) with کس as an indefinite 'person/one'. بودست is an archaic past copula (بود + است fused) meaning 'was' (past state). یزدان‌پرست is a compound noun meaning 'God-worshipper / devout person' and functions as the predicate complement describing that person.
segments.s648-g138: نیاورد is a negated past transitive verb 'did not bring / did not incur' (used idiomatically here 'did not suffer/meet with'). در + عهد here means 'in the age/epoch of' (idiomatic locative for a historical period). شاهان is the plural 'kings' marked by no case ending; شکست is the noun 'defeat' and is the direct object of نیاورد (i.e. 'did not meet defeat').
segments.s648-g139: فرستادمش is the 1st-person past verb 'I sent' plus enclitic object -مش 'him/her/it' (archaic orthography). نامه is the direct object 'letter'. پندمند is an adjective meaning 'admonitory / full of advice' modifying نامه; the adjective follows the noun in the normal Persian order here.
segments.s648-g140: دگر here means 'another / different' (modifying عهد). عهد is 'pledge / covenant' (object of reference). آن شهریار بلند is a nominal phrase 'that lofty king/sovereign' with آن 'that' as determiner, شهریار 'sovereign/king' as head, and بلند 'lofty' as a post-positive adjective (poetic placement). The whole segment qualifies what the letter concerned.
segments.s648-g141: برو is the imperative 'go' or an interjectional 'begone'. خوار بود is a predicative phrase 'was despised/held worthless', with خوار 'despised' and بود the past copula. آنچ (an archaic spelling of آنچه) = 'that which / what', and گفتم here is the speaker's verb 'I said' (1st-person past); سخن 'speech / words' completes or comments on 'what I said' — word order is poetic and compressed: literally 'Go; that-which-I-said was despised (as mere) speech.'
segments.s648-g142: هم here means 'also / moreover' (linking an additional admonition). اندیشه is a verbal noun 'thought / reflection' used imperatively/optatively in the sense 'consider / reflect upon'. روزگار کهن is a genitive phrase 'the ancient/course of old times' (روزگار 'time/fate', کهن 'ancient') specifying the object of reflection.
segments.s648-g143: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. او 'he' is the subject. کینه‌ور گشت is a verb phrase 'became vindictive' (کینه‌ور adjective + گشت 'became' = past change of state). و is the coordinating 'and'. من چاره‌جوی is 'I [was] a seeker of remedy' (چاره‌جوی a deverbal noun/adjective 'one seeking a remedy'); the copula is implied in poetic compression.
segments.s648-g144: سپه را uses را as the definite object/ topical marker: 'the host/army'. چو here again = 'when / as'. روی اندر آمد به روی is an idiomatic, poetic repetition meaning 'came face to face' — the first روی 'face/side' with اندر آمد 'came into', then به روی 'towards the face/opposite face' (so the phrase denotes an encounter). آمد is past 'came'. Word order is inverted for poetic emphasis.
segments.s648-g329: همان is a demonstrative meaning 'that/the very' referring back to a previously mentioned person; موبد موبدان is a compound/title meaning 'mōbed of mōbeds' (chief priest), with the repeated موبد functioning as title + genitival or intensifying element; اردشیر is the proper name in apposition. Word order is noun phrase (demonstrative + title + name).
segments.s648-g330: اسیران is the noun 'the captives/prisoners'; که introduces a relative clause 'who/that'; بودند is past plural 'they were'; برنا و پیر are coordinated adjectives 'young and old' (برنا = youthful, پیر = old), describing the captives. The clause literally = 'the captives who were young and old.'
segments.s648-g331: که here resumes the relative clause 'who'; از جنگ is the ablative 'from the war'; برگشت is the past intransitive 'returned' (3rd person); پیروز و شاد are predicative adjectives 'victorious and glad'. The sequence describes the subject's state upon return.
segments.s648-g332: گشاده شد is a two-word verbal notion 'was opened/was loosed' (گشاده = 'open/loosed', شد = past auxiliary 'became/was'); از بند پای قباد is a prepositional phrase 'from the fetter of Qobad's foot' where بند = 'fetter/chain' and پای قباد = 'Qobad's foot'. Together: 'he was freed from the fetter on Qobad's foot.'
segments.s648-g333: بیاورد is a past-tense narrative verb 'he brought' (object 'them' is implied from prior context); و = 'and'; اکنون = 'now'; ز جیحون گذشت = 'crossed/passed the Jeyhun' (ز + river name + گذشت past 'passed'). The two verbs narrate successive actions.
segments.s648-g334: ز ایران = 'from Iran' or 'of Iran'; سپاهست is a fused poetic copula meaning 'it is a host' or 'a great host is (present)'; بر کوه و دشت = 'upon mountain and plain' (بر = on/over). The clause states that a large army from Iran is spread over the mountains and plains.
segments.s648-g335: خروشی is an indefinite noun 'a roar/uproar'; ز ایران = 'from Iran'; برآمد is the past intransitive 'arose/issued forth'; که introduces a result/consequence clause; گوش begins that result clause as 'the ear'. The segment sets up cause (roar) and the following effect clause.
segments.s648-g336: تو is the 2nd-person subject 'you'; گفتی here functions as 'you said' (note: the provided sense-list does not include 'said', so the exact sense index is unavailable below and marked -1); همی is an archaic/intensive particle (here functioning as an aspectual/intensifying element, 'indeed/so'); کر = 'deaf'; شود = 'becomes/will become' (future/resultive in the content of reported speech); زان خروش = 'from that roar'. The clause: 'you said that the ear would become deaf from that roar.'
segments.s648-g65: فراز آمدش is an inverted, poetic word order: فراز (on high / up / toward him) + آمدش (آمد + ـش = 'came to him/it'), so literally 'up/above came to him'. تیغزن is a postposed noun meaning 'swordsman; sword‑wielder' used collectively; صد هزار quantifies it. The clause describes a mass arrival, with آمدش (past) as the main verb.
segments.s648-g66: همه جنگجوی is a noun phrase 'all (همه) warriors (جنگجوی)' modifying the preceding group; از در is a prepositional phrase 'from the gate/entrance of' (از = from, در = gate/door) and کارزار is the noun 'battle'. Together the phrase identifies the warriors' origin: 'all warriors from the gate of battle.'
segments.s648-g67: درم داد is a simple past clause: درم (dirham, money) + داد (gave) = 'he gave money.' The conjunction و links a second clause: آن لشکر آباد کرد has آن لشکر (that army/host) as object/topic, آباد کرد is a causative/resultative periphrasis ('made [them] prosperous/settled them') with کرد as the past tense auxiliary meaning 'caused/made.'
segments.s648-g68: دل مردم کینه‌ور is a noun phrase in fronted position: دل (heart) + مردم (of the people) + کینه‌ور (vindictive) — 'the people's vindictive hearts.' The predicate شاد کرد (made glad) follows, with کرد again as causative 'made.' This is a typical Persian SOV ordering where the object/topic precedes the verb.
segments.s648-g69: فرستاده‌ای خواند uses the object‑then‑verb order common in classical Persian: فرستاده‌ای (a messenger / a sent one) + خواند (called/summoned; past tense). شیرین‌زبان postposes an adjectival epithet 'sweet‑tongued' modifying the messenger. خواند here is the past tense 'call/summon' rather than 'read.'
segments.s648-g70: خردمند و بیدار و روشن‌روان is a string of coordinated adjectives modifying the messenger: خردمند (wise), بیدار (awake/alert), روشن‌روان (bright‑souled/cheerful). The repeated و (and) simply links the adjectives in series; there is no separate verb in this segment because they continue to describe the summoned messenger.
segments.s648-g71: یکی نامه بنوشت is an older/poetic past tense construction: یکی ('someone' or 'a certain one') + نامه ('a letter') + بنوشت (archaic/poetic past 'wrote', here functionally 'wrote a letter'). پر داغ و درد is a predicate/adjective phrase with پر ('full (of)') governing the nouns داغ ('burning grief') and درد ('sorrow'), so 'a letter full of grief and pain.' Note: the provided sense list for بنوشت does not match 'wrote', so that word is marked -1 below.
segments.s648-g72: دو دیده پر از آب uses a simple descriptive structure: دو (two) + دیده (eyes) + پر از (full of) + آب (water/tears) = 'his two eyes full of water (tears)'. The following و links the second coordinated description رخسار زرد (countenance pale), where رخسار (cheek/face) + زرد (pale/wan) describes his appearance. The adjectives follow the nouns as is normal in Persian.
segments.s648-g313: فرستاد is a past-tense verb ‘sent/dispatched’ (third-person). یکسر is an adverb meaning ‘straightaway/at once’ and modifies فرستاد. سوی is the preposition ‘toward’, so the clause literally reads ‘sent at once toward Sofzāy’ with the proper name سوفزای as the destination placed after سوی (typical Persian prepositional order).
segments.s648-g314: به دست introduces the agent/responsibility phrase literally ‘into the hand (of)’ and here functions like English ‘in the charge of / by the hand of’. یکی مرد is an indefinite noun phrase ‘a certain man’ (یکی = ‘a certain / someone’, مرد = ‘man’). پاکیزه‌رای is an adjective (poetic/compound) ‘pure‑minded / of pure opinion’ modifying مرد and follows it, as is normal in Persian adjective-noun order in poetry.
segments.s648-g315: چو is the subordinating conjunction ‘when’. لشکر is the subject ‘the army/host’. بدیدند is the past plural ‘they saw’. روی قباد is the direct object ‘the face (or presence) of Qobad’ (روی = ‘face’, proper name follows). Word order is standard: conjunction – subject – verb – object.
segments.s648-g316: ز here introduces the cause/result and is best read ‘because of / from’ (classical/poetic use). دیدار او = ‘the sight/seeing of him’ (دیدار used as ‘seeing’ or ‘meeting’), انجمن گشت is a verbal phrase ‘the assembly became’ (گشت = ‘became’), and شاد = ‘glad’. The clause literally: ‘because of seeing him, the assembly became glad.’
segments.s648-g317: بزرگان همه is a noun phrase ‘the great men, all’ (بزرگان = great men/elders; همه repeats ‘all’ for emphasis). خیمه بگذاشتند is the past plural verb phrase ‘they set up/placed tents’ (بگذاشتند = they placed/put up). In heroic/epic Persian the object (خیمه) precedes the verb as here.
segments.s648-g318: همه (again ‘all/everyone’) is subject, دست بر آسمان is the phrase ‘hands toward/on the sky/heaven’ (بر = ‘on/upon/toward’), and داشتند is the past auxiliary ‘they had’ used here with the sense ‘they held/raised’ (literally ‘they had [their] hands [on/upon] heaven’ = ‘they raised their hands to heaven’). The clause describes the elders’ gesture.
segments.s648-g319: که here introduces the content clause/explanatory clause (‘for/that’). پور شهنشاه is a nominal compound ‘the son of the shahanshah (emperor)’ (پور = son/of the line of), را is the object marker attached to that noun, and بی‌گزند is an adjective meaning ‘unharmed/without injury’ describing the son. The phrase thus reads ‘that the emperor’s son (was) unharmed.’
segments.s648-g320: بدیدند is the verb ‘they saw’ (past plural) continuing the narrative. با هرک is a prepositional phrase ‘by/with everyone’ or ‘before everyone’ (با = ‘with/by’, هرک = ‘whoever/everyone’ in classical usage). ارجمند is an adjective ‘esteemed/precious’ describing how he was regarded. The intervening بد is an archaic/poetic element here: it does not function with its ordinary negative sense and is best understood as an intensifying or idiomatic particle in this phrase; the whole unit expresses that he was highly esteemed by all.
segments.s648-g193: یکی here means 'someone/a certain one' and functions as an indefinite agent before the verb. تیغ زد is a verb + instrument construction literally 'struck (with) a sword' (تیغ as the instrument placed before زد). بر سرش marks the target 'on his head' (ش = 'his'), and the proper name سوفزای at the end identifies who is meant — the person whose head was hit.
segments.s648-g194: سپاه is the subject 'the army/host'. اندر آمد is a two-word verb phrase 'came in/arrived', and the adverbial phrase به تندی ز جای modifies it ('with haste/from their place') — به + noun gives an adverbial nuance ('by/with ...'), and ز جای is a poetic 'from the place'.
segments.s648-g195: بجست is a past action 'leapt/sprang' with its logical subject omitted (understood from context); از کف = 'out of the hand/palm' (کف = palm/hand), and تیغزن خوشنواز is a genitive/epithet 'the swordsman Khushnavaaz' indicating whose hand it was.
segments.s648-g196: انداخت is a transitive past 'threw/caused to fall' with اسب as its object. به شیب اندر is a directional phrase 'into/on(to) the slope' (به + شیب gives direction), and از فراز 'from on high' specifies origin/height — overall '(he) threw the horse down the slope from above.'
segments.s648-g197: بدید is simple past 'he saw'. آنک is a relative/connecting particle 'that/which', introducing the clause شد روزگارش درشت — شد is 'became', روزگارش 'his fate/fortune' (with possessive -ش), and درشت is a predicate adjective 'harsh/rough' describing the change in his luck.
segments.s648-g198: عنان را uses را as the definite object marker on عنان ('the reins'). بپیچید is past 'he twisted/turned' (prefix ب- here is part of the verbal form), و بنمود 'and he showed/revealed' (بنمود = showed/turned to display), with پشت 'back' as the thing shown — idiomatically 'he turned his back' (i.e. fled/retreated).
segments.s648-g199: چو is the simile particle 'like/as'. باد دمان is a poetic image 'the wind's hem/skirt' (a phrase evoking a sudden gust or trailing rush), از پسش means 'from behind him' (پسش = 'behind him'), and the proper name سوفزای follows for emphasis: 'Sofzāy'.
segments.s648-g200: همی‌تاخت is an imperfective/past-continuous 'was charging' or 'rushed on'. با نیزه marks the instrument 'with a spear'. سرگرای is an archaic/adjectival modifier indicating his engaged/eager manner (an old poetic word used adverbially here) describing how he charged.
segments.s648-g201: بسی کرد زان نامداران اسیر is a compact, somewhat inverted clause: بسی کرد (lit. ‘did much’) here means ‘made many’ or ‘took many’; زان names the group (‘of those’); نامداران اسیر is the noun phrase ‘renowned ones (نامداران) made captives (اسیر)’. The verb کرد is the past tense 'he made/caused' and applies to the following object phrase.
segments.s648-g202: بسی کشته شد هم بپیکان و تیر is a passive report: 'بسی کشته شد' = 'many were killed' (کشته شد = was killed/was slain); هم adds 'also'. The phrase بپیکان و تیر gives the instruments (by spear/arrow); the auxiliary شد marks the passive/result.
segments.s648-g203: همی‌تاخت is an imperfective/past iterative poetic form meaning 'he was charging' or 'he kept charging'; تا ... رسید links the duration/limit ('until he reached'); پیش لشکر = 'in front of the army/host'; رسید is simple past 'arrived/reached'.
segments.s648-g204: This clause reports what he saw: بره بر here functions adverbially (an archaic/poetic locative/phrasing, effectively 'all around' / 'on the plain'); بسی کشته و خسته is a coordinate noun phrase 'many slain and (many) exhausted/weary'; دید is simple past 'he saw'.
segments.s648-g205: ز بالا نگه کرد is a prepositional phrase + verb: ز بالا = 'from above/on high' and نگه کرد = 'he cast a look/turned his gaze' (کرد as past 'did'). پس introduces what follows in sequence: پس خوشنواز names the next actor — خوشنواز functions here as an epithet or name ('the melodious/pleasant‑sounding one').
segments.s648-g206: سپه را is the definite direct object with the particle را ('the host/army'); به هامون نشیب و فراز is a locative/directional phrase: به = 'toward/over/in', هامون = 'plain', and نشیب و فراز = 'lows and highs' (a fixed pair meaning 'hollows and heights'). The whole means Khushnawāz looked over the host across the plain's ups and downs.
segments.s648-g207: همه دشت sets the topic 'the entire plain'; پرکشته is a compound/adjectival participle meaning 'full of the slain' or 'many‑slain'; و خواسته attaches another adjectival idea 'and the desired/sought' (an adjective/participle) describing the state of the plain.
segments.s648-g208: شده is the perfect/passive participle 'has become' or 'was made'; دشت repeats the noun for emphasis ('the plain had become'); چون چرخ آراسته is a simile: چون = 'like', چرخ = 'the sky/heavens', آراسته = 'adorned', so 'had become adorned like the heavens.'
segments.s648-g49: This is a simple nominal clause with the subject/name 'جهاندیده' followed by a prepositional-origin phrase 'از شهر شیراز' and the past copula 'بود' at the end. Word order is typical Persian (subject → source phrase → copula); 'بود' is the past-tense copula 'was'.
segments.s648-g50: Two predicate epithets 'سپهبددل و گردن‌افراز' are joined by 'و' and finished again with the past copula 'بود', which here refers back to the same subject in the previous segment. These are adjectival/epithetical nouns describing the subject; the repeated 'بود' is a poetic device to balance the line.
segments.s648-g51: 'هم او' places emphasis on the same person already mentioned ('he himself' / 'also he'); 'مرزبان' is the head noun 'border-guard / frontier governor' and 'بزابلستان' names the region. The short adjective 'بد' appears between the title and the place as an archaic epithet—positioned like an adjective before the place name.
segments.s648-g52: The verb 'ببست' is past tense 'he closed/secured' (classical/poetic past form) and takes the following place names as its objects; they are coordinated by 'و' listing the regions 'بغزنین و کابلستان'. The verb comes at the end, consistent with Persian verb-final order, and the subject is the previously named 'او / مرزبان'.
segments.s648-g53: The clause opens with the temporal conjunction 'چو' = 'when', followed by the noun 'آگاهی' (information/news) and the verb 'آمد' (came). 'سوی سوفزای' is the directional phrase 'to Sofzay'; the verb 'آمد' is simple past. Together: 'When news came to Sofzay...'.
segments.s648-g54: The preposition 'ز' marks source 'from (Piruz)'; 'بی‌رای و بی‌رهنمای' are coordinated negative adjectives built with the privative prefix 'بی-' meaning 'without', describing 'پیروز'. Grammatically this whole phrase supplies the source and a descriptive qualifier of that source.
segments.s648-g55: This is a compact poetic clause: 'ز مژگان' marks the source 'from the eyelashes', 'سرشکش' (a small teardrop) is the noun, 'به رخ' gives the direction 'onto the face/cheek', and 'برچکید' is the past verb 'fell/dripped forth'. The verb-final order and the picturesque compounds ('سرشکش') are typical of Ferdowsi's style.
segments.s648-g56: 'همه جامه پهلوی بردرید' is a past-tense clause where 'همه' ('all/everyone') is the subject, 'جامه پهلوی' ('the side garments' / 'garments at the flank') is the object phrase, and 'بردرید' is the past verb 'tore'. The construction expresses collective action (they tore their garments) with the verb at the end as expected in Persian.
segments.s648-g233: اگر is the conditional marker 'if'. بازجویی is a noun 'questioning/interrogation' serving as the instrumental activity. ز + راه is a poetic prepositional phrase 'by/through the way/route' (preposition before noun), and ردی is an adjective after راه meaning 'worthless/vile' describing that way.
segments.s648-g234: بدانی is the 2nd-person singular present/subjunctive/epistolary form of دانستن used here as the apodosis ('you know / you would know'). که introduces the content clause. آن is the demonstrative 'that', کار is the head noun 'deed/act', بد is the adjective 'bad/evil' following the noun (classical word order), and ایزدی is an adjectival qualifier 'divine / in the sight of God' modifying کار.
segments.s648-g235: نه negates the following verbal phrase (here understood as 'was not ...'). بر باد شد is the idiom 'to be blown away / to be lost' (بر + باد + شد), with شد as the past copula/auxiliary. کشته precedes the name as a past-participial adjective 'the slain', and پیروزشاه is a proper name ('Pirooz-Shah' or 'the victorious king').
segments.s648-g236: کز introduces a relative/causal clause 'who/for whom'. اختر is the subject 'star' and سرآمد is used predicatively (archaic use meaning 'rose/came to head' or 'was foremost'). بدو is the dative/enclitic 'to him', and سال و ماه is a coordinated temporal phrase 'year and month' indicating the time-aspect assigned by the star.
segments.s648-g237: گنهکار شد is a copular construction: گنهکار 'sinner' + شد 'became/was'. زانک is a causal conjunction 'because/since' linking to the reason clause that follows. بشکست is the past-tense verb 'broke' (classical verb before its object), and عهد is the object 'oath/pledge'.
segments.s648-g238: گزین کرد is a light-verb construction: گزین 'choose' with the auxiliary کرد to make the past 'he chose'. حنظل is the direct object 'colocynth / bitter thing'. بینداخت is a past verb 'threw away/cast off' and شهد is its object 'honey/sweetness'—the line sets up a contrast (he chose bitterness and cast away sweetness).
segments.s648-g239: کنون 'now' sets the temporal frame. بودنی is a substantive/gerund 'that-which-is / what was to be' and is followed by the copula بود 'was'. و links to بر ما گذشت where بر marks the experiencer ('on/us') and گذشت (past of گذشتن) means 'passed/ befell'—so the clause says the destined thing existed and then happened to us.
segments.s648-g240: خنک is a predicate adjective 'pleasant/refreshing' placed before the relative clause. آنک is an archaic relative 'that which'. گرد here functions adverbially/verbally with گذشته (the past/gone) and نگشت is the negative past of گشت 'to turn/return'—together meaning 'that which, once past, did not turn back' (i.e. did not recur).
segments.s648-g17: نخستین is an adverb meaning “first.” به پندش is an instrumental/benefactive phrase (به + پندش “by/with his advice” or “by advising him”); the object is implicit and understood. توانگر کنم is a causative/result complement with کردن in the first-person subjunctive/optative (کنم) meaning “I will make (him) wealthy.”
segments.s648-g18: چو introduces the conditional clause here as “if.” نپذیرد is the negative present subjunctive/indicative meaning “he does not accept/submit.” از خونش is a ablative/genitive phrase “from his blood” (خون + possessive -ش). افسر کنم uses افسر (helmet) as a noun object with کردن (کنم) to mean “I will make (a) helmet,” so the whole clause reads “if he does not accept, I will make a helmet from his blood.”
segments.s648-g19: هرآنگه که is a temporal/conditional compound meaning “whenever” or “whensoever.” زین لشکر is a poetic phrase pointing to the army/host in question; زین here is classical/poetic and does not match the literal listed senses (see note). دین‌پرست is an adjectival compound “religion-worshipping / pious (as a group)."
segments.s648-g20: بنالد is a present tense verb meaning “moans/complains/laments.” بر ما is the prepositional phrase “against us.” یکی زیردست is a nominal phrase “one (a certain) underling/subordinate” acting as the person who laments; note classical word order places the actor at the end.
segments.s648-g21: دل مرد bakes the genitive relationship typical of Persian poetry (the heart of the man); بیدادگر qualifies the man as “tyrannical/cruel.” بشکنم is the first-person present-subjunctive/optative of شکستَن used here as a future/active threat: “I will break (his) heart.”
segments.s648-g22: همه is “all” modifying the coordinated phrase بیخ و شاخش (“his root and branch(es)”), where شاخش = شاخ + possessive -ش “his branch(es).” ز بن is a prepositional/ablative phrase “from the root/base,” and برکنم (بر + کنم) is the first-person form of an action verb meaning “I will pluck up / uproot / remove.” The sequence is object + origin phrase + verb, a normal poetic order.
segments.s648-g23: مباشید is the negative imperative/jussive plural (“do not be” / “you (pl.) must not be”). گستاخ is the predicate adjective “impudent/insolent.” با پادشا is a prepositional phrase “toward/with the king,” i.e., addressing behavior toward the monarch.
segments.s648-g24: بویژه is “especially.” کسی is “someone / someone in particular.” کو here is the archaic relative/pronoun equivalent to modern که (“who/that”) used in classical Persian (not supplied in the provided sense list). بود is the copula “is/was” used in verse as the linking verb, and پارسا is the adjective/noun “pious.” The clause means “especially anyone who is pious.”
segments.s648-g305: This is a compact past-tense clause: فرستاده (the messenger) + هم (also) + a temporal prepositional phrase در زمان (after a while) followed by the verbal phrase گشت باز. گشت (past of گشتن) here means 'turned/returned' and باز (again) reinforces the return. Word order places the time phrase before the verb, which is common in classical Persian.
segments.s648-g306: بیامد is a past tense verb 'came'. گرازان is a collective/plural noun 'a troop of boars' and بر here means 'against/upon' (indicating direction or attack). خوشنواز is a person-description 'the musician' (literally 'pleasant-player'). The clause thus reads literally 'boars came upon the musician.'
segments.s648-g307: بگفت is past-tense 'he said/told' (3rd sing.). آنچ بشنید is a fused relative clause 'what he had heard' (آنچ = 'that which', بشنید = 'he heard'). وزو here is an archaic contraction of و ز او (and from it / because of it) rather than the lexical sense listed — it functions as 'and because of that'. گشت شاد = 'became joyful' (گشت = became/turned, شاد = happy).
segments.s648-g308: همانگاه is an adverb 'immediately'. برداشت is past tense of برداشتن 'he took up / lifted' (here treated as the verbal sense 'took up'). بند in a poetic context means 'stanza/verse' (not a fetter). قباد is the proper name 'Qobad' and appears after the object, a common order in classical Persian where the subject may follow verb and object.
segments.s648-g309: همان modifies خواسته: 'that same/very desire'. The phrase سر به سر is an idiomatic intensifier meaning 'throughout / all over' (literally 'head to head'), and گرد کرد is a compound verbal idea 'went about / searched' (گرد here as 'to go about' with کرد as the light verb). The construction means 'he searched everywhere for that desire.'
segments.s648-g310: کجا introduces a rhetorical question 'where'. یافت is past 'found'. از + خاک و دشت (from the earth and the plain) gives the spatial domain; نبرد ('battle') here likely qualifies 'دشت' as 'the plain of battle' or stands appositionally — overall: 'Where could he find it among earth and the battlefield plain?' The clause is a questioning continuation of the preceding search.
segments.s648-g311: A noun phrase: همان تخت 'that same throne' + با تاج 'with the crown' + پیروز شاه 'the victorious king' (پیروز modifies شاه). The phrase groups items: 'the throne with the crown of the victorious king.' Word order places modifiers after the nouns in classical Persian.
segments.s648-g312: چو introduces a simile/comparison 'as/when'. چیز پراگنده is 'a scattered thing' (چیز used generically), and آن سپاه 'that army' follows; the clause reads 'when/as that army was scattered like a thing.' The adjective پراگنده describes the army; classical Persian allows this flexible order for poetic effect.
segments.s648-g73: This short clause places the prepositional phrase and the object before the verb in typical Persian order: به نامه درون (in the letter / within the letter) + پندها یاد (the counsels/reminders) + داد (gave/put). یاد داد here is a light-noun + verb compound meaning 'gave as a reminder' or 'set down/recorded'. داد is a simple past-tense main verb ('he gave/put').
segments.s648-g74: ز introduces the source or topic of the admonitions ('concerning / about'); the sequence lists the names of kings (proper nouns) joined by و. The phrase functions as the complement specifying whom the counsels referred to.
segments.s648-g75: وزان پس is a temporal sequence ('and from that / after that'), followed by the simple past verb فرستاد ('he sent'). نزد is used as a preposition meaning 'to / into the presence of' (nominative/oblique phrase), so the whole segment means 'after that he sent (it) to Balash.'
segments.s648-g76: که introduces reported speech or the content of what was sent ('saying/that'); شاها is a vocative ('O king!'), تو is the clitic personal pronoun 'you'. از + noun (از مرگ) gives the cause/reason ('because of / about death'), غمگین is an adjective 'sorrowful', and مباش is the negative imperative of بودن ('do not be'). The clause is a prohibitive admonition quoted after فرستاد.
segments.s648-g77: که again introduces the subordinate clause. این مرگ is fronted as the topical object ('this death'), followed by the nominal subject هر کس ('every/any person') and the predicate نخواهد چشید. نخواهد here functions as a future auxiliary with negative force ('will not / cannot'), and چشید (perfective past stem) is used as the lexical verb meaning 'to taste/experience' — together conveying 'there is no one who will not experience this death.'
segments.s648-g78: This clause states a moral prescription. شکیبایی و نام are two coordinated nouns ('patience and (one's) name/fame'). باید is an impersonal/modal verb meaning 'it is necessary / one must', followed by گزید (past stem of گزیدن) used here as the infinitival/lexical complement meaning 'to choose' — structure: [thing(s)] باید گزید = 'one must choose (patience and reputation)'.
segments.s648-g79: This clause starts with ز باد آمده, a poetic expression literally 'that which came by the wind' (ز + noun + past participle), with آمدِه (past participle) qualifying something brought by the wind. باز گردد is a jussive/future-form verb phrase 'may/might return' (باز = again, گردد = turn/return). بدم is a short possessive form meaning 'my harm/my misfortune' or figuratively 'my fault/ill‑fate' and functions as the subject/topic whose return is envisaged. Overall the clause is conditional/speculative.
segments.s648-g80: یکی is an indefinite subject 'one / someone'; داد here is a noun meaning 'justice' used as the complement of خواندش (the verb 'he called it / he named it'), so یکی داد خواندش literally 'one called it justice.' The conjunction و links to the ellipted verb for the next clause, with دیگر ستم meaning 'another (called it) oppression'. The verb خواندش (past) takes the enclitic object -ش 'it'.
segments.s648-g369: بدو is the prepositional pronoun 'to him/unto him' placed before the reporting verb. گفت here is the past tense 'he said' (the provided senses list does not include this verbal sense). شاهی 'kingship' is the noun being referred to, and نرانی is an archaic/poetic qualifier meaning 'manly' or 'manfully' (here modifying شاهی). همی is the old frequentative/continuative particle (used in epic Persian) giving an ongoing or emphatic shade to the verb phrase.
segments.s648-g370: بدان here is composed of the prefix ب- + دان (to know) functioning as 'that (thing)' or 'know that' in speech; را is the definite/object marker attaching to the phrase. ز نیکان = 'from the good (men)' (ز used in its poetic 'from' sense). ندانی is the present negative 2nd-person form 'you do not know'. همی again is the frequentative/emphatic particle, here adding an ongoing emphasis to ندانی.
segments.s648-g371: همی is the archaic frequentative/continuous particle placed before the verbal clause. پادشاهی 'kingship' is the theme/thing being acted upon. به بازی 'as a play' (به + بازی) is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in play / as a game'. کنی is the 2nd-person singular present-subjunctive/form (you do / you treat), used here to report the person's habitual action.
segments.s648-g372: ز is the poetic preposition 'from / because of'. پری وز in the metrical text is the split form of the proper name Parivaz/Parviz (here functioning as the source/reason); the two word-parts represent that name. بی‌نیازی is a nominal/adjectival phrase 'lack of need; self-sufficiency', and کنی (again 2nd-person) means 'you make / you cause' — so the whole says you make yourself without need because of Parivaz (i.e. you become carefree/indifferent).
segments.s648-g373: قباد is the personal name 'Qobad'. از تو is the usual comparative marker 'than you' (از used for comparison). در کار 'in affairs' is a locative/semantic phrase specifying the field. داناترست is the comparative adjective 'dānā + tar + ast' fused in classical poetry: 'is wiser / more learned'.
segments.s648-g374: بدین is the compressed form of به + این 'by/with this' or 'in this (respect)'; here it points back to 'this kingship'. پادشاهی 'kingship' names the office. تواناترست is the comparative of توانا 'able' (+ -تر + است), 'is more able / more capable' — again fused as is common in verse.
segments.s648-g375: به ایوان خویش اندر آمد is a standard verbal phrase: به ایوان 'to the iwan/porch', خویش 'his own' (formed from خوی + -ش), اندر آمد 'entered in / came inside' (اندر آمد = entered). The proper name بلاش appears after the clause as the subject (common in poetry to place names after verbs).
segments.s648-g376: نیارست here means 'he did not venture / it was not fitting for him' (an impersonal or negative-potential sense — the exact lexical item is archaic/poetic). گفتن is the infinitive 'to say' functioning as the complement of نیارست. که is the subordinator 'that', ایدر 'here', and مباش is the negative imperative 'do not be' (literally 'be not here' — quoted speech: he did not dare to tell someone 'do not be here' or more naturally 'stay here').
segments.s648-g353: همی‌بود is a past-continuous/iterative copular verb (classical 'همی' + 'بود') meaning 'there was' or 'continued to be'. جشنی is a noun 'a feast/celebration' (the patient/subject of 'همی‌بود' in this verbless-style clause). نه بر آرزوی : نه negates the following prepositional phrase; بر here functions as 'for/on' in the sense 'for (the) wish' → 'not for a mere wish'.
segments.s648-g354: ز introduces a causal/instrumental phrase 'by/through' (poetic use). تیمار is 'care/solicitude' and functions as the noun governed by ز. پیروز is an epithet 'victorious' modifying the following epithet آزاده‌خوی, which here is used as a descriptive epithet 'magnanimous/free‑spirited' attached to the person who caused the feast.
segments.s648-g355: همه modifies چامه‌گر to give 'all (the) poets' as subject. سوفزا را uses را as the definite object marker marking Sofza as what is praised. ستود is a simple past verb 'praised' with همه چامه‌گر as subject and سوفزا را as object.
segments.s648-g356: ببربط is an elided/prepositional phrase meaning 'with the barbat' (با بربط) and sets the musical accompaniment. همی is the classical continuous/iterative particle (here emphasizing the act of singing). رزم ترکان is a genitive phrase 'the battle(s) of the Turks'. سرود is a past-tense verb 'he sang/recited/composed' (the singer implied by context).
segments.s648-g357: مهان را: مهان 'guests' with را here functioning as a topic/emphasis marker ('as for the guests'). همه quantifies 'all the guests'. چشم بر is an idiomatic construction 'eyes on' = 'gaze upon', followed by the proper name سوفزای (Sofzā) as the object of their gaze.
segments.s648-g358: ازو (از + او) indicates cause/agent 'from him / because of him'. گشته شاد is a compound meaning 'became glad' (گشته = become/turned + predicate شاد 'happy'). و links the next clause; بدو 'to him' is the indirect object, داده رای is a noun-verb cluster 'gave (داده) counsel/opinion (رای)' meaning 'they gave him their advice/consent'.
segments.s648-g359: همه شهر ایران is a collective phrase 'all the towns/cities of Iran' (همه quantifying شهر ایران). بدو is the indirect object 'to him'. گشت here is the simple past/preterite 'turned' or 'returned', and باز is the adverb 'again' modifying the verb.
segments.s648-g360: کسی را که introduces a relative clause; in this context کسی functions with negative force ('no one') and که = 'who/that' links the clause. بد کینه is a two-word description 'ill‑hearted / with hatred' (بد modifies کینه here: 'with spite'). خوشنواز is an adjectival/noun predicate 'melodious' or 'skilful musician' describing that (non‑existent) person; the clause is elliptical (typical in epic poetry) so the copula or main verb is omitted but the sense is 'there was no one who, out of malice, was a skilful player.'
segments.s648-g289: چنین گفت is a fixed sequence meaning “thus he said” (چنین = thus/so + گفت = said, past). کاین is an archaic fused form of که + این meaning “that this” or “that which is this”; ایزدی بود uses بود (past of “to be”) to state that something “was divine” (ایزدی = divine/sacred). و بس is a short colloquial/poetic phrase meaning “and that is enough” or “and no more.”
segments.s648-g290: This clause is a single sentence: جهان بد سگالد نگوید بکس. جهان بد = “the world (when bad / ill‑treating).” سگالد is an older/poetic verbal form meaning “has harried/assailed” or “has set dogs on / troubled” (here passive/affective sense). نگوید is the verb meaning “does not say/tell” (negative present/pathetic formula), and بکس (بَکس) = “to/for anyone / any person” (here indefinite object). Together: “One whom the world has ill‑assailed tells no one.”
segments.s648-g291: بزرگان ایران = “the great ones / nobles of Iran” (بزرگان plural noun + ایران). که هستنـد اسیر = a relative clause “who are captives”: که = who/that, هستند = are (3rd‑person plural present), اسیر = captive. Word order is noun + relative clause, standard in Persian poetry.
segments.s648-g292: قبادست here functions syntactically as a single unit indicating possession or relation to Qobad (in prose one would read قباد است “is Qobad’s / belong to Qobad”); با = “with”/“together with”; نامدار اردشیر = “the renowned Ardashir.” Note that قبادست in the wordlist is given as a clothing term, but in context it is the fused form قباد + است and should be read as relating the captives to Qobad.
segments.s648-g293: دگر هر که دارید = “anyone else / whoever else you have”: دگر = other/else, هر که = whoever, دارید = you have (present). بر نای بند is a short instructive phrase: بر + نای + بند; نای = reed/pipe (literal) and بند = bond/fetter (or the verbal root “to bind”), so in context it instructs to bind/fasten (i.e. put in bonds). The precise idiomatic sense is ‘put into bonds’ or ‘bind (them)’. Word order places the directive phrase after the relative phrase.
segments.s648-g294: فرستید is the imperative or optative/command form “send” (from فرستادن). سوی = “toward / to”; منش ارجمند = “the noble/esteemed man” (منش here used in a sense of person/character, modified by ارجمند ‘esteemed’). Together: an instruction to send the bound persons to that noble figure.
segments.s648-g295: دگر خواسته هرچ دارید نیز = “further/other requests, whatever you have, also”: دگر modifies خواسته (an additional request); هرچ دارید = “whatever (thing) you have” (هرچ = whatever, دارید = you have); نیز = also/additionally. This is a noun phrase listing further demands.
segments.s648-g296: ز دینار وز تاج و هرگونه چیز enumerates the kinds of recompense: ز (poetic preposition = از) = “of/from”; دینار = dinars/money; وز here is a poetic linking particle (written و + ز) meaning roughly “and/of” (the wordlist senses are literal but here it functions as a connective); تاج = crown/sovereignty; و = and; هرگونه چیز = any kind of thing. The phrase lists money, crown, or anything else as acceptable.
segments.s648-g1: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when' placed before the verb; بنشست is 3rd person past 'he sat (down)'. با + noun (سوگ ماهی بلاش) is a prepositional phrase 'with the mourning of …' — ماهی here is poetic for a beautiful person/beauty and بلاش is the proper name Balash (no ezafe is written because of the poetic register).
segments.s648-g2: سرش is the possessed noun 'his head' (سر + possessive ش). پر ز is a compound meaning 'full of' (پر + ز 'from/of'), so سرش پر ز گرد = 'his head full of dust' (گرد = dust). و links the two coordinated predicates; رخش here denotes countenance/face and پرخراش is a compound adjective 'full of scratches' (پر + خراش).
segments.s648-g3: سپاه آمد is a simple past clause 'the army came/arrived'. و connects the following nominal phrase; موبد موبدان is a poetic construct meaning 'mobed of mobeds' — i.e. the chief priest (a genitive/intensifying repetition common in epic diction) standing apposed after the verb.
segments.s648-g4: هر آنکس که بود is a free relative 'every one who was' (هر ... که introduces 'whoever'). از marks membership 'from among' (از رد و بخردان = 'from among the ...'); بخردان is the plural agent 'the wise ones'.
segments.s648-g5: فراوان modifies the verb as an adverb 'plentifully/many [things]'; بگفتند is past 'they said/spoke'. با او marks the addressee 'with/to him', and ز پند indicates the matter/source 'about advice' or 'words of counsel'.
segments.s648-g6: سخنها is the plural subject/object 'words/speeches'. که introduces a relative clause 'which/that'; بودی is a past-form verb 'were' here ('which were'); ورا is the indirect object 'to him'; سودمند is the predicate adjective 'useful' — so 'words that were useful to him.' Note the verb appears before the indirect object due to poetic word order.
segments.s648-g7: بران is a locative/adverbial 'on that/upon it'; تخت شاهیش is 'his royal throne' (تخت + adjectival/possessive شاهی or شاهیش); بنشاندند is the past plural 'they seated/installed [him]'. The object 'him' is implied and understood from context.
segments.s648-g8: بسی here functions as a distributive/adverbial 'much/many' modifying زر و گوهر 'gold and gems'. برافشاندند is past plural 'they scattered/showered' — i.e. they showered out plentiful gold and jewels (a ceremonial lavishness).
segments.s648-g225: تبیره is the subject (here treated as a proper name); برآمد is a simple past/punctual verb 'issued forth/rose' placed after the subject (typical SOV flexibility in poetic diction). ز + پرده‌سرای is a prepositional phrase 'from the curtain-room/stage' giving source; پرده‌سرای is a compound noun used in poetic register.
segments.s648-g226: نشست is the past verb 'sat' / 'dismounted' (here transitive/intransitive sense of alighting); از بر باره is a sequence of prepositional phrases literally 'from upon the load/burden' meaning 'off the pack'; بر سوفزای 'onto Soofzāy' marks the destination (Soofzāy is a proper name). Word order places the verb before its following locative phrases, a flexible poetic arrangement.
segments.s648-g227: فرستاده‌ای is an indefinite noun formed from the past participle 'فرستاده' plus the indefinite/indefinite-like ending 'ـای', meaning 'a messenger' (one who was sent); آمد is the simple past 'came'; از introduces the origin 'from Khushnavaz' (خوشنواز here functions as the source/agent name or epithet 'the skilled musician').
segments.s648-g228: به نزدیک is a prepositional phrase 'to/near the presence of'; سالار is the head/chief and گردن‌فراز is an epithet/apposition meaning 'proud' (here a title-like adjective following the noun). The whole phrase marks the messenger's destination.
segments.s648-g229: که introduces a subordinate content clause ('that' / 'so that' / reported content). از introduces a list of sources: جنگ و پیکار are near-synonyms 'war and battle', and خون ریختن uses the verbal noun (مصدر) ریختن 'shedding/spilling' with خون to mean 'blood‑shedding'. The phrase enumerates violent causes or actions.
segments.s648-g230: نباشد is a negative third‑person form meaning 'there should be no / let there not be' (classical/poetic negated predication); جز از is the idiom 'except from / except for'; رنج و آویختن are two nominalized actions (رنج 'suffering/hardship' and آویختن the masdar 'hanging' or 'being hanged'), listing what alone will occur. The construction contrasts the forbidden (war, bloodshed) with the sole outcomes.
segments.s648-g231: دو is the numeral 'two' modifying مرد 'men'; خردمند is an adjective 'wise' and نیکو گمان is a compound epithet 'of good opinion/repute' (nِیکو qualifies گمان, forming a descriptive phrase). The grouping gives a compact adjectival description before the following verbal clause.
segments.s648-g232: به دوزخ is the directional prepositional phrase 'to hell'; فرستیم is a first‑person plural verb 'we sent' (in classical Persian this form can be past or jussive depending on context, here rendered as a past narrative 'we sent'); هر دو روان is the object 'both souls' (هر دو = 'both', روان = 'soul'), specifying that both were sent. Word order places the destination phrase before the verb, a common poetic arrangement.
segments.s648-g129: This is a fronted indirect-object + comment + verb + subject sequence: به مهتر (to the chief) marks the addressee, چنین گفت (said thus) places the manner before the verb, and مرد دبیر (the man, the scribe) is the subject coming after the verb — a common poetic/inverted order instead of the more prosaic مرد دبیر چنین گفت به مهتر.
segments.s648-g130: که introduces a reported/clausal complement ('that …'). این نامه is the topic 'this letter'. پر + noun here means 'full (of)'; the copula is folded into تیغست where تیغ + -ست (archaic/poetic contraction of است) yields a predicate; the series گرز و تیغ و تیر lists instruments of violence. So syntactically: 'that this letter is full of mace and sword and arrow.'
segments.s648-g131: شکسته شد is a passive/resultative past (literally 'became broken' / 'was broken' / 'was humbled'). آن مرد جنگ‌آزمای is the definite subject: آن 'that/the', مرد 'man/warrior', جنگ‌آزمای 'battle‑tried' (an epithet). Word order places the verbal phrase before the subject for rhetorical effect.
segments.s648-g132: ازان (= از آن 'from that' / 'by that') links back to the preceding clause; پر سخن is a compound adjective 'full of words' (i.e. wordy/verbose); نامه سوفزار is a genitive construction 'the letter of Sofzar' (noun + possessor). Together: 'by that wordy letter of Sofzar.'
segments.s648-g133: هم ('also') + اندر زمان زود ('within the proper/early time' = promptly) is a temporal adverbial; پاسخ نبشت is a past negative (نبشت = نَ + نوشت 'did not write'). The overall sense: 'Also, he did not quickly/within the time write a reply.'
segments.s648-g134: سخن هرچ ('whatever words' — هرچ as a free relative 'whatever') + بود اندرو ('were in it'; اندرو = اندر + او = 'in it') gives the clause 'whatever words were in it'; خوب و زشت 'good and bad' lists the character of those words. Note classical order: predicate (بود) precedes locative (اندرو) for emphasis.
segments.s648-g135: نخستین ('first, firstly') + چنین گفت ('thus he said') is an index of sequence and speech. کز here functions as a subordinating particle introducing the content of what was said ('that/that from'), followed by کردگار ('the Creator/God') as the source or object of fear in the following clause.
segments.s648-g136: بترسیم is a hortative/subjunctive first‑person‑plural form 'let us fear / we should fear'. وز is the fused classical particle (و + ز = 'and from/of') introducing the second object; گردش روزگار is a fixed phrase meaning 'the turning/revolutions of fate' (گردش = turn/rotation, روزگار = fate/times). So: 'Let us fear the Creator and the turning of fortune.'
segments.s648-g185: هوا (the air/sky) is the grammatical subject; دام کرکس شد literally ‘became a trap (for) the vulture’ with شد as the past copula 'became'. از پر تیر is an instrumental/causal phrase ‘by/from the arrows' feathers’ (پر تیر = ‘feathers of arrows’), where از marks source/instrument in poetic diction.
segments.s648-g186: زمین شد is a predicative clause 'the ground became…' with شد as past 'became'. ز (poetic form of از) introduces the cause/source: ز خون سران = 'from the blood of the heads'; آبگیر is the predicate noun 'a pond/pool', so overall 'the earth became a pool because of the heads' blood.'
segments.s648-g187: ز هر سو is a fronted locative 'from/on every side'. ز مردان تلی کشته بود is a verbless-looking clause where تلی (a heap/mound) and کشته (slain, a past participle functioning adjectivally) together with بود ('was' — past) report the state: 'there was a heap of slain men' (i.e. 'heaps of slain men lay on every side').
segments.s648-g188: کرا is an archaic relative/pronominal particle 'who/which'; از جهان روز برگشته بود is a verbal phrase with برگشته بود (past perfect/return-state) meaning 'had returned from the world' — a poetic idiom for 'had departed this life' (i.e. was dead). از marks source ('from the world').
segments.s648-g189: بجنبید here functions as a past/state verb 'was/were stirred/moved' (classical narrative use), and بر قلبگه سوفزای is a locative phrase 'in/on the heart-seat of Sofzāy' with بر marking the location. The clause describes an internal stirring in Sofzāy's heart.
segments.s648-g190: یکایک ('one by one') modifies سپاه ('the host/army'); اندر آمد is the past 'came in/advanced' with اندر marking motion/position 'into/among', and ز جای 'from their places' (ز = from, جای = place) gives source of their coming.
segments.s648-g191: وزان ('and/from that') is a conjunctive/temporal particle 'thereupon' or 'from that moment'. با تیغ کین خوشنواز groups the instrument with two modifiers: تیغ کین 'sword of vengeance' and خوشنواز 'sweet-sounding' (an epithet used poetically); خوشنواز postmodifies the noun phrase.
segments.s648-g192: بپیچید is a past-tense verb 'he turned/twisted' (performed a wheeling/turning movement). و آمد 'and came' continues the action; به marks direction toward the compound location تنگی فراز—'the narrow (tangy) high place'—so the clause means he turned and advanced into the narrow high/pass area.
segments.s648-g265: کزیشان is the fused poetic form of کز + ایشان, here functioning as 'from them' or 'on their account' (archaic/poetic کز = 'from'). ازین پس is the temporal adverb 'from this on / henceforth' (از + این + پس). نجوییم is ن + جوییم (first-person plural) — a negated present/subjunctive sense 'we will not seek / we shall not seek'; جنگ is the object 'war' that follows the verb in this poetic word order.
segments.s648-g266: به ایران is the goal phrase 'into / to Iran' (به here = 'towards/into'); بریم is first-person-plural subjunctive/optative 'let us take / we will take' from بردن used in the sense 'lead/take (an army)'; این سپه is the direct object 'this army/host' (این = 'this' + سپه = 'army'); بی‌درنگ is an adverb 'without delay / immediately' modifying بریم and placed at the end for emphasis.
segments.s648-g267: که introduces a relative/clarifying clause 'who/that'; در دست ایشان بود is an idiom 'was in their hand(s)' meaning 'was under their control / captive to them' (در = 'in', دست = 'hand' meaning 'power', ایشان = 'they', بود = past 'was'). کیقباد is the proper name placed at the end as the clause's referent.
segments.s648-g268: چو here means 'as / like'; فرزند is 'son / offspring'; پیروز is an epithet 'victorious' (qualifying the following name); خسرو نژاد is a compound meaning 'of the stock/lineage of Khosrow' (خسرو = the name Khosrow, نژاد = 'race / lineage'), so the phrase describes resemblance to a scion of Khosrow the Victorious.
segments.s648-g269: همان is an emphatic demonstrative 'that very / the same'; موبد موبدان is a title-formal phrase 'mobad of mobads' (a chief priest — repetition forms a superlative/title); اردشیر is the personal name in apposition. Word order places the title before the name for emphasis.
segments.s648-g270: ز is the poetic form of از meaning 'from / out of'; لشکر بزرگان = 'the host/army of great ones' (لشکر = 'army/host', بزرگان = 'great ones / nobles'); برنا و پیر lists the constituents 'the young (برنا) and the old (پیر)', joined by و. Together it says he came from a host composed of both young and old.
segments.s648-g271: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. جنگ سازیم uses سازیم (from ساختن) with جنگ to mean 'we make/prepare war' — an idiom for 'we wage war'; با + خوشنواز means 'with Khushnawaz' (خوشنواز here is a personal epithet/name; context indicates a person rather than literally 'pleasant-player').
segments.s648-g272: شودکار is a poetic compound (شود + کار) meaning 'the matter will become' or 'it will turn into a case' — شود is 'it becomes/will become' and کار = 'affair / matter'. بی‌سود = 'useless / fruitless'; بر ما = 'for us / upon us' (shows who suffers); دراز = 'long' — together: 'the affair will become fruitless and long for us' (i.e., it will drag on to our harm).
segments.s648-g273: کشد is the narrative/present verb meaning 'kills' that governs the following relative clause. آنک (آن که) introduces that relative clause meaning 'who/whom'; دارد is the present tense of داشتن 'has/holds' and takes the following noun phrase as its object. ز ایران is a poetic locative 'from Iran' and اسیر is a noun 'a captive'—together: 'who he has as a captive from Iran.'
segments.s648-g274: This is the appositive/subject: قباد (the name) with the epithet جهانجوی 'world‑seeking' attached; چون is the simile marker 'like/as', and اردشیر is the name used for the comparison. There is no copula — the name and epithet are juxtaposed and modified by the simile.
segments.s648-g275: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. نیستی is second‑person singular negative 'you are not' (here meaning 'you are not present'); در میانه قباد is a locative phrase 'in the middle/midst of Qobad' (i.e. among Qobad's company).
segments.s648-g276: ز موبد 'from the mobed' gives the source. نکردی is the past negative second‑person of کردن 'you did not do/you did not take (as a learning)'; دل و مغز are coordinated objects 'heart and mind' (i.e. inner feelings and understanding); یاد functions as 'remembrance/learning'—together: 'you did not learn/take to heart and mind from the mobed.'
segments.s648-g277: گر is a conditional 'if'. او را is the marked pronoun 'to/for him' (experiencer marked with را). ز ترکان 'from the Turks' gives the source of the ill; بد آید literally 'bad comes' — 'misfortune happens to him.' بروی is the verb 'he goes/withdraws' (third person singular present/future in epic style), expressing the subsequent action.
segments.s648-g278: نماند is third‑person negative 'will not remain/there will not be left'. به ایران marks location 'in Iran'. جز از = 'except from' (introduces the sole remainder). گفت و گوی is a compound meaning 'speech/talk and discourse'—so: 'nothing will remain in Iran except talk.'
segments.s648-g279: یکی here is an indefinite 'a certain' / 'it would be (one)'; ننگ 'shame/disgrace' is the predicate; باشد is the subjunctive/optative form of 'to be' (expressing 'would be' or 'is to be'); که introduces the subordinate clause, and تا رستخیز means 'until the Resurrection' (رستخیز = resurrection/uprising).
segments.s648-g280: بماند is the present/subjunctive 'remain' (that something should remain). میان introduces the locus 'among/between'; دلیران is the plural 'the brave/valiant ones'; ستیز is the noun 'strife/struggling' — together: 'that strife remain among the brave.'
segments.s648-g145: This clause ends with the past copula/verb شد (became) and a predicate adjective آشفته (disturbed). The prepositional phrase به پیروز بر اختر is a poetic qualification: 'the star (or the star that is) for the victor' — the noun اختر (star) is qualified by پیروز (victorious) using به to mark the relation. Word order is inverted for poetic effect (qualifier + noun + predicate + شد).
segments.s648-g146: نه negates what follows: نه برکام من = 'not according to my wish' (برکام = 'according to desire/fortune', من functioning as a possessive 'my' in poetic usage). The clause شاه تو کشته شد places the title شاه before the pronoun تو (vocative/apposition), and کشته شد is a passive past ('was killed'). Together: 'not to my wish — my king, you were killed.'
segments.s648-g147: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. بشکست is a past verb 'broke' and takes the direct object پیمان شاهان ('the covenant of the kings'), where شاهان follows پیمان as a genitive plural. داد here functions as the noun 'justice' (i.e. the idea of right/order) placed after the event for emphasis.
segments.s648-g148: نبود is the past negative existential 'there was not'. از introduces the source/partitive ('from/out of his youth'), with جوانیش = 'his youth' (جوانی + -ش enclitic). یک روز شاد is the noun phrase 'a single happy day' (one day + adjective). Literal order 'there was not, from his youth, a single happy day.'
segments.s648-g149: نیامد is the negative past 'did not come'. پسند ('approval, liking') is the thing that 'did not come' and جهان‌آفرین ('world‑creator', i.e. God) qualifies پسند: 'the Creator's approval did not come.' The verb precedes its qualifying noun phrase here for poetic inversion.
segments.s648-g150: تو گویی که is an idiomatic expression meaning 'it is as if' or 'one would say that' (تو is the colloquial expletive and گویی here introduces an evidential/simile). بگرفت is past 'seized/took hold of' and پایش ('his foot' = پای + -ش enclitic) is the object; زمین ('earth') is the agent/scene. The clause reads 'as if the earth seized his foot.'
segments.s648-g151: هر آنکس که introduces a generalizing relative clause 'whoever'. عهد نیا is a compound meaning 'ancestral/forefathers' oath' (عهد = oath, نیا = ancestor). بشکند is the present/subjunctive form used for general conditional/prospective sense 'breaks' or 'should break' — here translated as habitual/general 'breaks.'
segments.s648-g152: سر راستی ('the head of truth/uprightness') is the direct object, with را marking definiteness/emphasis. بپای افگند is a compound verbal phrase meaning 'threw down at the feet' — افگند is the past 'threw/cast' and بپای (poetic contraction of به پای) indicates the destination 'to the feet.' Together: 'casts the head (i.e. honor) of truth to the feet.'
segments.s648-g33: چنان دان is an imperative phrase: دان (know) with چنان 'thus/so' sets the manner or content of what you should know. که introduces a subordinate/result clause ('so that' / 'such that'). نادان‌تری is a comparative adjective 'more ignorant' functioning as the predicate of the subordinate clause, and آن زمان is an adverbial phrase 'at that time / then'.
segments.s648-g34: مشو is the negative imperative (from شدن) 'do not become'. بر ... بر introduces the prepositional phrases that mark the relation of suspicion: بر تن خویش = 'toward/on your person' and بر بدگمان = 'into suspicion / be suspicious'. خوی + ش is the noun with the enclitic suffix -ش (his/her/one's own), here meaning 'your own disposition/person'.
segments.s648-g35: وگر is a conditional 'if / but if'. کار بندید uses بندید (here a perfective/past or result-state sense 'is/has been tied/closed') to mean 'if the affair is settled/entangled'. پند مرا is a noun phrase 'p advice / counsel' with مرا representing the first‑person clitic ('to me' or 'my' depending on construction) — here marking that the counsel is mine or addressed to me.
segments.s648-g36: سخن گفتن is a verbal noun / infinitive 'speaking' (literally 'the saying of speech'). سودمند is a predicate adjective 'useful/beneficial'. مرا here attaches to سودمند and functions as the dative/possessor (literally 'useful to me' or 'my speaking is useful'), with را appearing as part of the pronominal clitic in older word order.
segments.s648-g37: ز introduces source or 'from among'. شاهان داننده is a noun phrase 'the kings (who are) knowing/knowledgeable' (classical Persian often allows modifiers after the noun). یابید is a past (3rd person plural) form 'they found/obtained', and گنج is the direct object 'treasure/wealth' which here follows the verb in the classical order.
segments.s648-g38: کسی را with the negative verb ندیدم yields 'I saw no one' ('کسی' here functioning as 'anyone/no one' in negative contexts). ز دانش 'from/by knowledge' marks the source/means; ندیدم is the perfective past 'I did not see'; به رنج uses به + noun to mean 'by/with toil' (an adverbial of manner). The clause states the speaker's observation (or lack of it) that no one, by toil, gained from knowledge.
segments.s648-g39: مهتران is the plural subject 'the chiefs/elders'. آفرین خواندند is a compound predicate 'they uttered praise / they cried “bravo”' (خواندند is past 3rd person plural). برو here is a short, archaic/discourse particle placed before the subject — in context it functions as a connective 'thereupon/then' (a poetic usage rather than the literal verb 'go').
segments.s648-g40: ز introduces the cause or source 'because of / from'. دانایی او 'his wisdom' is the noun plus the pronoun او. فرو ماندند is a compound verb: فرو is an intensifying/adverbial element 'deeply / far', and ماندند is past 3rd plural 'they remained / were left' — together 'they were left far behind (by his wisdom)'.
segments.s648-g361: بدان پهلوان دل همی شاد کرد is an independent clause meaning 'he made that champion's heart rejoice.' بدَان here is an archaic/poetic locative/target phrase ('to/at that' or 'thereupon') introducing 'پهلوان' ('that hero'); دل is the patient (object) marked implicitly; همی is the continuous/iterative verbal particle (here 'kept' / simple poetic presentive) and کرد is the past/simple perfective verb 'made/did' taking شاد ('glad') as its complement. Word order is poetic: target (بدان پهلوان) + object (دل) + adverbial particle (همی) + predicate (شاد کرد).
segments.s648-g362: روان را ز اندیشه آزاد کرد is a coordinated clause 'and he freed the soul from thought.' روان را is the definite object (را marks روان), ز indicates separation/'from', اندیشه is the source/cause (here 'anxiety, care, or thought'), آزاد is the predicate adjective 'free', and کرد is the past tense verb 'made/caused' (= 'freed'). The structure is 'object (روان را) + prepositional source (ز اندیشه) + predicate (آزاد) + verb (کرد)'.
segments.s648-g363: ببد سوفزای از جهان بی‌همال is a predicative clause: بَبد is an archaic/past copular verb 'was' (past tense), سوفزای is the proper name (subject), از جهان is a genitive/locative phrase 'in/of the world', and بی‌همال is an epithet 'without equal/peerless' modifying the name. Word order places the copula before the name (poetic inversion).
segments.s648-g364: همی‌رفت زین گونه تا چار سال means 'he continued (to be) in this manner for four years.' همی‌رفت is the imperfect/continuous past 'kept going/continued', زین گونه ('in this way') is an adverbial phrase (زین here = 'this' in archaic/poetic use, گونه = 'manner'), تا چار سال is a temporal phrase 'for four years/ until four years passed.'
segments.s648-g365: نبودی جز آن چیز کو خواستی is a negative-existential clause: نبودی is past 'there was not' (or 'was not'), جز آن چیز = 'except that thing', and کو خواستی is an archaic relative construction ('which you wanted' — کو here functions as an archaic relative/pronominal linker). The phrase as a whole = 'there was nothing but that thing which you wished.'
segments.s648-g366: جهان را به رای خود آراستی is a transitive clause 'you arranged/ordered the world to your own will.' جهان را (object, marked by را), به رای خود = 'according to your counsel/will' (به رای = 'by/in the opinion/plan', خود = emphatic/possessive 'your own'), and آراستی is the past verb 'you adorned/arranged' (here 'arranged/ordered'). Word order is object + adverbial + verb, typical in Persian poetry.
segments.s648-g367: چر فرمان او گشت در شهر فاش is a temporal/conditional clause: (چرا/چر here functions as a temporal/conditional conjunction 'when/whenever') فرمان او گشت = 'his command became' (گشت = 'became/turned'), در شهر فاش = 'publicly/in the city' (فاش = 'revealed/open'). The clause means 'when/whenever his decree became known in the city.'
segments.s648-g368: به خوبی بپرداخت گاه از بلاش is the main result clause: به خوبی بپرداخت = 'he rendered/paid out good(kindness)' (به خوبی = 'with kindness/goodness', بپرداخت = past 'paid/gave/bestowed'), گاه = 'at times' or 'occasionally', از بلاش = 'by/through Balash' (Balash is a personal name). The verb بپرداخت takes به خوبی as manner and از بلاش as an agent/means.
segments.s648-g217: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when' (classical/poetic use). خورشید is the noun 'the sun' (subject). بنماید is the present/epic-form verb 'shows, displays' (ب‑ prefix + نما + د) and governs the following prepositional phrase; از چرخ دست is a poetic genitival phrase 'from the hand of the sky/heaven' (از = 'from', چرخ = 'sky/heaven', دست = 'hand' used metaphorically), placed after the verb for emphasis.
segments.s648-g218: برین is بر + ین = 'on/at this' (poetic form = 'بر + این'), دشت 'plain' is the locative; خیره is a participial/adverbial adjective 'staring/astonished' modifying the implied person; نباید نشست is an impersonal modal construction 'one must not sit / one should not remain' (نباید = 'must not/should not', نشست = infinitive/finite sense 'to sit/remain' in classical usage).
segments.s648-g219: به کین is a prepositional phrase 'by/for this vengeance' (به = 'for/by', کین = 'vengeance'); شهنشاه ایران شویم is the clause 'let us become shahanshah of Iran' with predicate شهنشاه ایران and the verb شویم (first-person-plural present-subjunctive/hortative 'let us become / we shall become') placed at the end, a typical classical Persian word order.
segments.s648-g220: برین دز is 'on/for this dze' (برین = 'on/this', here meaning 'in this raid/venture' in context); به کردار شیران is a prepositional phrase 'in the manner/conduct of lions' (به = 'in, as', کردار = 'deed/conduct', شیران = 'lions' used metaphorically for brave men); شویم repeats the hortative 'let us become' (same form and function as above).
segments.s648-g221: همه لشکرش is the subject 'his whole army' (همه = all, لشکرش = his army). دست بر برزدند is a verbal phrase meaning 'laid hands upon / struck' where دست ('hand') combines with the particle بر ('upon/at') and برزدند is the past plural perfective 'they struck/attacked' (classical past form).
segments.s648-g222: همی is an adverbial particle adding immediacy/emphasis ('then/indeed'); هر کسی = 'each person/every man' is the subject of the clause; رای دیگر زدند is an idiom 'they struck/adopted another plan' (رای = 'plan/counsel', دیگر = 'other/different', زدند = past plural 'they struck' used here idiomatically 'they took/formed'); overall: 'each man adopted a different counsel.'
segments.s648-g223: برین همنشان is a locative phrase 'among/on these companions' (برین = 'on/this', همنشان = 'those seated with/companions' used poetically); تا is the conjunction 'until'; ز خم سپهر is a poetic locative 'from the curve/bend of the sky' (ز = poetic 'from', خم = 'curve/bend' often meaning horizon, سپهر = 'sky/heaven').
segments.s648-g224: پدید آمد is the verbal phrase 'appeared/came into view' (پدید = 'manifest/appearing', آمد = 'came' — together = 'appeared'); آن زیور تاج مهر is a nominal apposition 'that ornament, the crown of Mehr' (آن = 'that', زیور = 'adornment/jewelry', تاج = 'crown', مهر here refers to the deity/solar divinity 'Mehr' or the sun's emblem).
segments.s648-g161: ز + میدان + خروشیدن is a short prepositional phrase 'from the field (mīdān) the roaring (khurūshīdan)'. ز is the poetic preposition 'from'; میدان (mīdān) is the location; خروشیدن is a verbal noun/infinitive 'to roar' used here as the noun 'roaring'. گاودم follows as the thing heard (a noun), placed after the verbal noun in poetic word order.
segments.s648-g162: شنیدند is the past tense, 3rd-person plural 'they heard'. و simply joins the next phrase. آوای is the noun 'the sound/voice' (construct with the following modifier). رویینه is an adjective 'iron-armored/metallic' modifying خم; خم here is used in its basic sense 'curve/bend' and in poetic context denotes a curved implement (the bow) or the metallic bending that makes a sound — together 'the sound of the iron-curved (bows)'.
segments.s648-g163: آورد is the main past verb 'he brought'. بکش here is used in a purposive/poetic sense 'to slay' (literally the root 'kill'); it modifies آورد to indicate purpose ('brought ... to slay the homeland'). میهن is the object 'homeland'. چنان سپاه 'such a host' quantifies the army; چنان is a degree adverb 'so/much'.
segments.s648-g164: که introduces a result clause 'so that...'. بر چرخ خورشید literal order 'on the wheel (charkh) the sun' — چرخ = the heavens/sky in poetic register. گم is 'lost' used predicatively, and کرد (here) means 'caused/made' (i.e., 'caused [it] to be lost'); راه 'path' is the thing lost. Note the slightly archaic/poetic inversion (verb before object) in 'گم کرد راه'.
